behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is the boundary: correctness depends on real model output quality (context-grounded specificity vs generic text). A stubbed model can always return a pre-grounded reply and hide grounding failures (MOCK-HIDE). No real OS/network/subprocess is required beyond the model call itself, so not System-only. Rejected Workflow because behavior text covers a single model-quality decision (“generated reply references specific points”), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM on representative email threads; assert the draft contains thread-specific entities/requests and fails if it stays generic despite those details being present.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is semantic contradiction detection between natural-language draft claims and KB facts; that typically depends on real AI/ML judgment, so mocks can pass while real detection fails (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Integration-only would be valid only for pure routing/retry plumbing, but this behavior is about correctness of contradiction flagging itself. Rejected Workflow because behavior text is one check outcome (“flags claims ... contradict entries”), not a multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model-backed consistency checking with drafts containing subtle contradictions to KB entries; verify contradictions are flagged and non-contradictions are not.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: This is a generation-quality behavior under missing evidence: whether the model abstains/disclaims vs hallucinates. Deterministic stubs hide this (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Integration would only test control flow (e.g., empty retrieval passed through), not the model’s abstention behavior. Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one generation decision under one condition, not multiple sequenced agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: With real LLM and retrieval returning no relevant KB items, assert the draft contains an explicit uncertainty/disclaimer and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/state-gating logic (approval state -> send action) rather than model output quality. Real AI/ML output is not required (LLM-DEP no). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required to catch the core failure; an in-process fake mail sender can prove no send before approval (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System because behavior text is about gate semantics (“blocks sending until ... approves”), which can be validated before any real transport call. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decision is required for pass/fail.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + approval state store + fake sender; attempt send without explicit approval and assert sender is not invoked, then approve and assert exactly one send invocation.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple steps in sequence, including agentic generation and fact-check decisions, plus cross-step state handoff (MULTI-STEP yes). This exceeds single-step Agentic and standard System boundaries; end-to-end failures can emerge only across the chain. Rejected Agentic because text explicitly demands the full pipeline (“full sequence ... works end-to-end”).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real pipeline on a real email thread: ingest thread, generate with real LLM, run real fact-check against KB, surface approval UI/state, and verify progression and outputs across all stages.
